H.R.3684 - Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act

Sponsor: Rep. DeFazio, Peter A. [D-OR-4]

Committees: House - Transportation and Infrastructure

This bill addresses provisions related to federal-aid highway, transit, highway safety, motor carrier, research, hazardous materials, and rail programs of the Department of Transportation (DOT).

Specifically, the bill authorizes $550 billion in new federal infrastructure spending over five years, including $110 billion for roads, bridges, and major projects.

The bill was signed into law by President Biden on November 15, 2021 as Public Law 117-58.

Key provisions include funding for broadband infrastructure, clean water investments, electric vehicle charging stations, and modernization of airports and ports.
